---
title: "CinnamonExports_R"
author: "sasandi nissanka"
date: "2025-12-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(writexl)
library(tseries)
library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)
library(stats)
library(lubridate)
```
```{r}
# Import the Excel file
data <- read_excel("C:/Users/HP/Downloads/monthly_export_summary.xlsx")
```

```{r}
data
```

```{r}
# Define test size (2 years = 24 months)
test_size <- 24
```

```{r}
# Split data into training and test
train_data <- data[1:(nrow(data) - test_size), ]
test_data  <- data[(nrow(data) - test_size + 1):nrow(data), ]
```

```{r}
# Save training data to Excel
write_xlsx(
  train_data,
  "C:/Users/HP/Downloads/cinnamon_export_train.xlsx"
)

# Save test data to Excel
write_xlsx(
  test_data,
  "C:/Users/HP/Downloads/cinnamon_export_test.xlsx"
)
```

```{r}
# rename
names(train_data)[4] <- "Total_Quantity_Tonnes"
colnames(train_data)
```

```{r}
# Convert train data to time series (monthly)
train_ts <- ts(
  train_data$Total_Quantity_Tonnes,
  start = c(2004, 1),
  frequency = 12
)
```

```{r}
# plot time series with train data

plot(
  train_ts,
  main = "Monthly Cinnamon Export Quantity (Train Data)",
  ylab = "Quantity (Tonnes)",
  xlab = "Year"
)
```

```{r}
acf(
  train_ts,
  lag.max = 65,
  main = "ACF - Train Series (65 Lags)"
)

pacf(
  train_ts,
  lag.max = 65,
  main = "PACF - Train Series (65 Lags)"
)
```

```{r}
# adf test in eviews with trend and intercept showed a deterministic trend (0.005 p value which is less than 0.05 threshold). And with intercept only and with no intercept and no trend adf tests showed stochastic trend having greater p values than 0.05. So first differencing should be done. adf tests after first differencing showed that deterministic trend and stichastic trend were removed
```

```{r}
# first differencing to remove the deterministic and stochastic trend
diff_train_ts <- diff(train_ts)
```

```{r}
plot(
  diff_train_ts,
  main = "First Differenced Train Series",
  ylab = "Differenced Quantity",
  xlab = "Year"
)
```
```{r}
# ACF & PACF of first differenced series to check seasonality

acf(
  diff_train_ts,
  lag.max = 65,
  main = "ACF - First Differenced Series"
)

pacf(
  diff_train_ts,
  lag.max = 65,
  main = "PACF - First Differenced Series"
)
```

```{r}
# there are spikes at each lag 12 so seasonal differencing is needed
```

```{r}
# seasonal differencing of the first differenced series

seasonal_diff_ts <- diff(
  diff_train_ts,
  lag = 12
)
```

```{r}
plot(
  seasonal_diff_ts,
  main = "Seasonally Differenced Series (d=1, D=1, s=12)",
  ylab = "Differenced Quantity",
  xlab = "Year"
)
```
```{r}
# acf and pacf after seasonal differencing

acf( seasonal_diff_ts, lag.max = 82, main = "ACF - After Seasonal Differencing on First differenced series" ) 

pacf( seasonal_diff_ts, lag.max = 82, main = "PACF - After Seasonal Differencing on First differenced series" )
```

```{r}
# p = 3 (PACF cuts off around lag 3)
# d = 1 (first differencing)
# q = 1 (ACF cuts off after lag 1)
# P = 1 (PACF shows significant negative spike at lag 12)
# D = 1 (seasonal differencing)
# Q = 1 (ACF shows significant negative spike at lag 12)
```


```{r}
model <- auto.arima(
  train_ts,
  d = 1,
  D = 1,
  seasonal = TRUE,
  stepwise = FALSE,
  approximation = FALSE
)

summary(model)
```

```{r}
checkresiduals(model)
```


```{r}
# Rename the 4th column
names(test_data)[4] <- "Total_Quantity_Tonnes"

colnames(test_data)
```

```{r}
# compare forecast with actual test data
# Forecast for test horizon
h <- length(test_data$Total_Quantity_Tonnes)
sarima_forecast <- forecast(model, h = h)

# Convert test data to time series
actual_ts <- ts(
  test_data$Total_Quantity_Tonnes,
  start = c(2023, 7),
  frequency = 12
)

# Extract forecast values
pred_values <- as.numeric(sarima_forecast$mean)

# Create comparison table
comparison <- data.frame(
  Month = as.Date(paste0(test_data$Period, "-01")),
  Actual = as.numeric(actual_ts),
  Forecast = pred_values
)


comparison
```

```{r}
# forecast accuracy

accuracy(
  sarima_forecast,
  actual_ts
)
```

```{r}
# actual vs forecast plot

ggplot(comparison, aes(x = Month)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Actual, color = "Actual")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Forecast, color = "Forecast")) +
  labs(title = "Cinnamon Export Forecast vs Actual on test data",
       y = "Quantity (Tonnes)",
       x = "Month") +
  scale_color_manual("", values = c("Actual" = "blue", "Forecast" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
# Forecast 3 months beyond the full dataset
future_forecast <- forecast(model, h = 3)

# Rename 4th column
names(data)[4] <- "Total_Quantity_Tonnes"

# Full time series
full_ts <- ts(
  data$Total_Quantity_Tonnes,
  start = c(2004, 1),
  frequency = 12
)

# Actual data frame
actual_df <- data.frame(
  Month = as.Date(paste0(data$Period, "-01")),
  Quantity = as.numeric(full_ts)
)

# Future forecast data frame
last_data_month <- max(actual_df$Month)
future_df <- data.frame(
  Month = seq(from = last_data_month %m+% months(1), by = "month", length.out = 3),
  Quantity = as.numeric(future_forecast$mean)
)

# Combine for plotting
plot_df <- rbind(
  data.frame(Month = actual_df$Month, Quantity = actual_df$Quantity, Type = "Actual"),
  data.frame(Month = future_df$Month, Quantity = future_df$Quantity, Type = "Forecast")
)

# Plot
ggplot() +
  # Actual line
  geom_line(data = actual_df, aes(x = Month, y = Quantity), color = "blue", size = 1) +
  geom_point(data = actual_df, aes(x = Month, y = Quantity), color = "blue", size = 2) +
  # Forecast line (dashed, connected to last actual)
  geom_line(data = rbind(tail(actual_df, 1), future_df),
            aes(x = Month, y = Quantity), color = "red", size = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(data = future_df, aes(x = Month, y = Quantity), color = "red", size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Cinnamon Export: Forecast for Next 3 Months",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Quantity (Tonnes)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
